{"key_event":"Union militancy and stagflation weaken state authority","reasoning":"Civil society remained extremely powerful: unions, regional nationalists, rights movements, and the enlarged electorate all constrained governments, though Northern Ireland violence and emergency powers damaged ordinary civic life in one part of the UK. State power stayed strong in administration, welfare provision, security, and regulation, but fiscal crisis, IMF dependence, EEC constraints, industrial unrest, and lost legitimacy in Northern Ireland reduced effective capacity and credibility.","society_change":0.0,"society_power":9.9,"state_change":-0.6,"state_power":8.8}
